observation_summary: The JXPath expression '$a + $b <= $c' fails to evaluate correctly when variables are involved, returning false instead of true. The issue specifically relates to how relational operations handle comparisons between non-Iterator and Iterator types.
hypothesis: The implementation of the relational operation incorrectly handles the case where the right-hand side (RHS) is an Iterator. The original code was calling 'containsMatch((Iterator) right, left)', which effectively swapped the arguments, leading to an incorrect comparison logic for the relational operator.
prediction: The fix will involve correcting the argument order passed to the 'containsMatch' method when the RHS is an Iterator, and potentially implementing the 'containsMatch(Object, Iterator)' method to correctly handle the comparison of a single value against an Iterator.
experiment_rationale: The provided diff shows that the call 'containsMatch((Iterator) right, left)' was changed to 'containsMatch(left, (Iterator) right)'. Furthermore, a new method 'containsMatch(Object value, Iterator it)' was added to correctly iterate through the RHS and perform the 'compute' operation, confirming that the original logic was procedurally incorrect.
reasoning_summary: The defect is a classic procedural error where the logic for comparing a value against an iterator was implemented incorrectly (swapping arguments). This is a computational/procedural flaw rather than a missing guard (Checking) or a simple value assignment error (Assignment/Initialization). Therefore, it falls under Algorithm/Method.

alternative_types.why_not_primary: While the fix involves changing a method call, the root cause is the internal logic of how the relational operation is computed, not a mismatch in the API contract between two components.
